zh.Cuenca, Castilla-La Mancha, España: 西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈昆卡
西班牙卡塔倫亞比斯巴特 德 萊里達
zh.lifeSummary: 當Pedro Ordoñes  García de Aza de Roa y Ayllon於1092年出生在西班牙時，他的父親Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera42歲，母親Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba18歲。他於1119年與María García de Villamayor在西班牙結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1233年7月11日享年141歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Pedro Ordoñes  García de Aza de Roa y Ayllon于1092年出生在西班牙时，他的父亲Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera42岁，母亲Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba18岁。他于1119年与María García de Villamayor在西班牙结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1233年7月11日享年141岁。

nameMeanings.name: Ordoñes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Hispanic (Ordoñes): altered form of Spanish Ordóñez (see Ordonez ).

variants.name: Ordonez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Juan, Jorge, Manuel, Mario, Jesus, Miguel, Cesar, Javier, Fernando.
Spanish (Ordóñez): patronymic from the medieval personal name Ordoño, of uncertain origin and meaning.

nameMeanings.name: García
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jesus, Carlos, Luis, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Mario, Raul, Roberto, Ramon, Rafael.  Portuguese Wenceslao, Joao, Paulo, Catarina, Albeiro, Ligia, Godofredo, Lidio, Wenseslao, Adao, Afonso.

Spanish (García) and Portuguese: from a medieval personal name of uncertain origin. It is normally found in medieval records in the Latin form Garsea, and may well be of pre-Roman origin, perhaps akin to Basque (h)artz ‘bear’. This is the most common surname in Spain and also one of the most common surnames elsewhere in Spanish-speaking world. In the US, where it is also found among Native Americans (mainly Pueblos in NM), it is the sixth most frequent surname. Compare De Garcia .

variants.name: Grassia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Amedeo, Maurizio, Pasquale, Rocco, Salvatore.
Italian: from a metathesized form of the personal nameGarsia, from Spanish García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Arcia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alberto, Francisco, Arturo, Dagoberto, Enrique, Jose, Ramiro, Adalberto, Aleida, Alejandro, Alfredo, Amalia.
Hispanic: probably an altered form of Spanish García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Garcie
meanings: Altered form, under the French influence in LA, of Spanish García (see Garcia ). This form of the surname of Spanish origin is also found in southern France.
Spanish (Ávila): variant of García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Garcilazo
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico and Argentina): altered form of Spanish (Valencia) Garcilaso, a compound of the personal name Garci (a short form of García, see Garcia ) and the nickname laso ‘weak, exhausted’.

variants.name: Gacia
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Garsia
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of   Garcia  .

variants.name: Gracia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Jesus, Luis, Guadalupe, Mario, Pedro, Javier, Jorge.

Spanish; Catalan (Gràcia): from a short form of the Christian religious epithet de la Gracia, meaning ‘of mercy’ and forming part of the Marian name Maria de la Gracia ‘(Holy) Mary of Grace’, from Spanish gracia, Catalan gràcia ‘grace, mercy’.
In some cases possibly also an altered form of Spanish García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: De Garcia
meanings: Spanish and Galician (De García); Portuguese: patronymic from Spanish and Galician García and Portuguese Garcia , with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Garces
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jorge, Carlos, Juan, Jose, Manuel, Cesar, Rafael, Luis, Fernando, Gustavo, Javier, Mario.  Portuguese Albeiro, Paulo.
Spanish; Catalan (Garcés); Portuguese (Garcês): patronymic from the personal name García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Garzia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Alfredo, Ernestina, Fernando, Juan, Pedro, Ricardo.  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Ciro, Corrado.
Italianized form of Spanish   Garcia  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Aza
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish: habitational name from a place called Aza or Haza in Burgos province. Compare   De Aza  .

Japanese: rare name from Fukushima prefecture usually written 呰 ‘slander’, but the character could be a miswriting of 此口 Konokuchi, another rare name meaning ‘this opening’. Another possibility also pronounced Aza could be 字 ‘(one from a) rural district’.

variants.name: De Aza
meanings: Spanish: variant of Aza , with the preposition de ‘from’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Roa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Carlos, Jose, Jorge, Pedro, Roberto, Fernando, Jesus, Juan, Alfredo, Arturo, Manuel.
Spanish: habitational name from Roa in Burgos province.

nameMeanings.name: Ayllon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish (Ayllón): habitational name from the Castilian town of Ayllón (Segovia).

variants.name: Ayon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Francisco, Efren, Guadalupe, Juan, Miguel, Consuelo, Manuel, Ramon, Rosario, Salvador.
Spanish (Ayón): variant of Ayllon .

nameMeanings.name: Pedro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Pedro Ordoñes  García de Aza de Roa y Ayllon was born in 1092, in Spain, his father, Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera, was 42 and his mother, Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba, was 18. He married María García de Villamayor in 1119, in Spain. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 11 July 1233, at the age of 141.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Pedro Ordoñes  García de Aza de Roa y Ayllon est né en 1092, à Espagne, son père, Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé María García de Villamayor en 1119, à Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 11 juillet 1233, à l’âge de 141 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Pedro Ordoñes  García de Aza de Roa y Ayllon 1092 in Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera 42 und seine Mutter Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba 18. Er hat 1119 in Spanien María García de Villamayor geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 11. Juli 1233 im Alter von 141 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Pedro Ordoñes  García de Aza de Roa y Ayllon è nato il nel 1092 nel Spagna, il suo padre, Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato María García de Villamayor nel 1119, nel Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 11 luglio 1233, all'età di 141 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Pedro Ordoñes  García de Aza de Roa y Ayllonが1092年にスペインで生まれた時，父Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájeraは42歳，母Condesa Ava Pérez de Trabaは18歳であった。1119年にスペインでMaría García de Villamayorと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1233年7月11日に141歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Pedro Ordoñes  García de Aza de Roa y Ayllon(이)가 1092년 스페인에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1119년 스페인에서 María García de Villamayor(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1233년 7월 11일 141세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Pedro Ordoñes  García de Aza de Roa y Ayllon nasceu em 1092, em Espanha, seu pai, Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com María García de Villamayor em 1119, em Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de julho de 1233, com 141 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Pedro Ordoñes  García de Aza de Roa y Ayllon родился в 1092 г. в местности Испания, его отцу по имени Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени María García de Villamayor в 1119 г. в местности Испания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 11 июля 1233 г. в возрасте 141 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Pedro Ordoñes  García de Aza de Roa y Ayllon nació en 1092, en España, su papá, Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba, tenía 18 años. Se casó con María García de Villamayor en 1119, en España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 11 de julio de 1233, a la edad de 141 años.
